preamble: The transformation of the uvular nasal [ɴ] to the velar nasal [ŋ] is an instance of fronting and is frequently driven by place assimilation. It is commonly observed in languages with moraic nasals or in environments preceding velar consonants.

examples.note: The moraic nasal /n/ (written as ん) is often realized as the velar nasal [ŋ] when it precedes velar consonants [k] or [ɡ].

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
